records.ety: [AS., from L. martyr, Gr. ma`rtyr, ma`rtys, prop., a witness; cf. Skr. sm to remember, E. memory.]

defs: One who, by his death, bears witness to the truth of the gospel; one who is put to death for his religion; as, Stephen was the first Christian martyr.
quotes: To be a martyr, signifies only to witness the truth of Christ; but the witnessing of the truth was then so generally attended with persecution, that martyrdom now signifies not only to witness, but to witness by death.

defs: Hence, one who sacrifices his life, his station, or what is of great value to him, for the sake of principle, or to sustain a cause.
quotes: Then if thou fall'st, O Cromwell, Thou fall'st a blessed martyr !

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Martyred (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Martyring.]

defs: To put to death for adhering to some belief, esp. Christianity; to sacrifice on account of faith or profession.
To persecute; to torment; to torture.
quotes: The lovely Amoret, whose gentle heart Thou martyrest with sorrow and with smart.
Racked with sciatics, martyred with the stone.
